entry.title: Re: Client-specific transport routing in Cloud ALM TMS
entry.content:
Hi, linhdh,
Yes, you can set your STMS so that Dev:100 -> QUA:100 and Dev:200 -> QUA: 200, etc.

In Cloud ALM, you set the Deployment Plan > System Groups with the desired development system to Dev:100, quality to QUA:100, production to the ??? (e.g. PRD:100) and another System Group with development set as Dev:200, quality as QUA:200, production as ???( PRD:200).

In your deployment plan/landscape, you can set the System groups:

 


And in Features, when creating Transports, you can select the source and target (screen after saving and the TRs are created on managed system:

On Quality (and Production), client 000 must have the Transports: Import use case activated so the import will trigger from Cloud ALM.  (KBA 3468539 - First Steps in using Change Deployment Management has a link to this blog which is a great guide to configuring all the use cases on the relevant clients and a wealth of information in using CDM in Cloud ALM.)

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: SAML-Based Single Sign-On Setup with SAP Identity Authentication Service
entry.content:
Introduction

About SAP Datasphere

SAP Datasphere is SAP’s unified data service for integrating, modeling and governing data across hybrid and cloud environments. As part of SAP’s Business Technology Platform (BTP), it supports enterprise-scale analytics, governance and collaboration.

 

Need for SSO

Traditional password-based authentication poses risks such as credential sprawl and inconsistent access control. Implementing SSO in Datasphere enhances:

  •              Security: Centralized identity and access management.
  •              User Experience: Seamless access without repetitive logins.
  •              Compliance: Alignment with enterprise security and governance standards.
  •              Efficiency: Reduced administrative overhead for user provisioning and password resets.

 

Architecture Overview

SSO in SAP Datasphere is typically implemented using SAML 2.0 (Security Assertion Markup Language) through SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication (IAS).

Components Involved

  • SAP Datasphere Tenant: The target system for SSO-enabled access.
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services: Identity Authentication (IAS): Acts as the trusted authentication broker.
  • Corporate Identity Provider (IdP): Provides user authentication.
  • SAP BTP Subaccount: Links Datasphere to IAS through service bindings.

 

Authentication Flow

 

Prerequisites

Before enabling SSO, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  1. SAP Datasphere Tenant - Provisioned and accessible via SAP BTP
  2. SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) - Tenant available and linked to BTP subaccount
  3. Corporate Identity Provider - Configured to support SAML 2.0
  4. Admin Access - Administrator roles in Datasphere, IAS and IdP
  5. Email Domain - Verified in IAS for trusted domain mapping

You must have the system owner role for your SAP Datasphere tenant to perform this configuration.

 

Configuration Steps

Step 1: Access IAS Tenant

  • Log in to your SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication admin console.
  • Under Applications & Resources → Applications, create a new application named “SAP Datasphere Test”.

Step 2: Download Metadata

  • Login to Datasphere tenant: System -> Administration -> Security
  • Authentication Method: SAML Single Sign On (SSO)
  • SAML Single Sign On (SSO) Configuration: Step1 - Download Service Provider Metadata

Step 3: Configure SAML Settings in IAS

  • SAML 2.0 Configuration: Define from Metadata -> Upload the metadata downloaded from Datasphere tenant

  • Subject Name Identifier

Source - Identity Directory and Value - Email

  • Attributes:

Maintain below values in Self-defined attributes.

For SAML attributes to be recognized it is also important that the “Groups” attribute contains the value of “sac”.

  • Conditional Authentication

Select “Default Identity Provider” - > Your IDP

“Check” the checkbox – Allow users stored in Identity Authentication service to login.

  • Download Metadata file from SAML 2.0 Configuration:

Step 4: Configure Trust in Datasphere

  1. In SAP Datasphere → System → Administration → Security Settings → Identity Provider Configuration.
  2. Upload Identity Provider Metadata -> Upload.

     3. Choose a user Attribute to map to your identity provider -> Email. It should map to User ID, Email or a custom attribute. The attribute will be used to map users from your existing SAML user list to SAP Datasphere. The selected user attribute must match the NameID (Subject Name Identifier) used in your custom SAML assertion.

4. Confirm that the mapping is working -> Verify Account

Copy the URL and open it in a private/incognito browser window to avoid reusing an existing session and ensure you are prompted for a fresh login.

Upon successful verification, the Login Credential field will be highlighted with a green border. After confirming this, select “Convert” to complete the SAML SSO configuration.

 

Step 5: Test Authentication

  1. Access SAP Datasphere via SSO-enabled URL.
  2. Verify that users are redirected to IAS and authenticated by the corporate IdP. 
  3. Ensure user attributes and roles are correctly mapped in Datasphere.

Best Practices

  • Use HTTPS for all communications between systems.
  • Enable Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) at the corporate IdP level.
  • Periodically rotate SAML certificates. 
  • Maintain audit logs in IAS for compliance.

Conclusion

Enabling SSO in SAP Datasphere strengthens enterprise security while enhancing usability and operational efficiency. Centralizing authentication through IAS and your corporate identity provider reduces the risk of credential misuse, simplifies onboarding, and improves user productivity. As organizations continue to adopt cloud and hybrid data platforms, a well-governed identity strategy becomes essential. This SSO implementation helps establish that foundation and supports future growth and integration initiatives.

SAP Sources: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/9f804b8efa8043539289f42f372c4862/9b26536159354aea9024a99cbbe60b4e.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/datasphere/4477056515.html?locale=en-US

entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

After the 2602 upgrade, we noticed that a Manual Rework activity is appearing in the Manage Your Solution app in the Production system, but it is not visible in the Quality/Test system.

When we click on Action Required, it shows that certain master data needs to be set up. However, this master data has already been maintained in both the Q and P systems.

Could you please advise:

  1. Is any action required from our side, even though the master data is already maintained?
  2. Why is this Manual Rework activity appearing only in Production after the 2602 upgrade?

For reference, we have attached the relevant screenshot.

Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Re: Standard App/Report for Weekly Production
entry.content:
Dear @SaiBhargav396,

Option 1: Best standard match for your columns is Display Production Order Confirmation (CO14)

  • CO14 is a standard Public Cloud app (CO14) to display production order confirmations
  • In CO14 you can filter by a date range and use the operation overview where work-center related fields and confirmation quantities are shown

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/4304b753128eb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

How it maps to your requested report:

  • Work Center from the confirmed operation (operation overview)
  • Material Produced typically the production order material (header material)
  • Quantity Produced yield/confirmed quantity
  • Date Produced confirmation/posting date of the confirmation

So if the business accepts produced date = confirmation date, CO14 is the cleanest standard solution

SAP Notes below:

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002810926

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003425074

Option 2: If you’re in Repetitive Manufacturing use Display Production Quantities (MF51)

  • MF51 is a standard app to search/view/download production quantities for a plant/MRP area for a given period weekly works well

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/166bb6531de6b64ce10000000a174cb4.html

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('MF51')/S36

IMPORTANT: If you need Date Produced = Goods Receipt posting date

Then CO14 alone may not satisfy you because it’s confirmation-based. A GR-based list will give you material/qty/date but usually not work center. To get both GR date and work center in one weekly report the typical standard approach in Public Cloud is Embedded Analytics (Custom Analytical Query) combining the relevant released data sources

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c0c54048d35849128be8e872df5bea6d/8573b810511948c8a99c0672abc159aa.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/custom-analytical-queries-as-an-alternative-for-lis-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13700408

Summary:

  • Start with CO14 for a fast weekly production report export
  • If the business wants a true weekly dashboard/reporting experience build a Custom Analytical Query on the standard VDM content

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: How to schedule jobs to run every 30 minutes instead of every 3 hours with batch-based triggers
entry.content:
Hi SAP Support, 

 

In the Manage Situation Types - Extended Application, the selection for recurrence interval for batch based job scheduling is currently only 3, 6, 12, 24 hours, but we require the interval time of 30 minutes. 

Can this 'Repeat Interval' of 30 minutes be chosen. 

 

Thank you. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to schedule jobs to run every 30 minutes instead...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Thank you for your query regarding the recurrence interval in Manage Situation Types – Extended Application.

 

For batch-based job scheduling, the available repeat intervals for the 'Daily' repeat type are system-defined. The supported values are 3, 6, 12, and 24 hours.

 

Intervals shorter than 3 hours (for example, 30 minutes) are not supported. This limitation is in place to ensure system stability and prevent potential performance impacts.

 

You can find additional information on batch job scheduling in the following documentation:

Batch Job Scheduling. 

 

Kind regards, 

SAP Support
